chunk_summary: Protein targets in pediatric CKD vary by stage and dialysis modality, emphasizing growth support, PEW prevention, and bioavailable protein intake adjustments for dietary patterns.
learning_objectives: Understand protein requirements for pediatric CKD stages 3–5 and dialysis modalities.
Learn strategies to manage PEW and optimize growth in children with CKD.
Identify dietary adjustments for lactovegetarian patterns in pediatric CKD.
Recognize the importance of documenting PEW and monitoring growth trajectories.
key_facts: Protein targets: 100–140% of healthy-child DRI for CKD stage 3; 100–120% for stages 4–5.
CKD stage 5D dialysis protein allowances: 0.15–0.3 g/kg/day extra for PD, 0.1 g/kg/day for HD.
Lactovegetarian diets may require 1.2–1.3 times higher protein intake than mixed diets.
PEW documentation predicts adverse outcomes and prompts earlier intervention.
Correct metabolic acidosis when bicarbonate is persistently <22 mmol/L before considering protein reduction.
reference_quote: Protein prescriptions in pediatric CKD are written to support growth while accounting for uremia and dialytic losses. (Reference 1)
document_key_topics: Protein targets by CKD stage and dialysis modality
Prevention and management of PEW in pediatric CKD
Dietary adjustments for bioavailable protein intake
Growth monitoring and escalation plans in CKD
